---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# dfx

<!-- badges: start -->
[![Lifecycle: experimental](https://img.shields.io/badge/lifecycle-experimental-orange.svg)](https://lifecycle.r-lib.org/articles/stages.html#experimental)
[![CRAN status](https://www.r-pkg.org/badges/version/dfx)](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=dfx)
<!-- badges: end -->

dfx is a data.frame transformation package with a tidy-like interface but written in base-R without non-standard evaluation. 
Designed as a lightweight and stable dependency for R package development.

## Installation

You can install the development version of dfx like so:

``` r
# install.packages('remotes')
remotes::install_github("selkamand/dfx")
```

## Quick Start

```{r paged.print=TRUE}
library(dfx)

# Select columns
mtcars |>
  bselect(c("mpg", "hp"))

# Rename Columns
mtcars |>
  brename(c("miles_per_gallon" = "mpg", "horsepower" = "hp"))
```
